evidence: Prisma models AuditLog, Experiment, ExperimentAssignment, ExperimentResult define @@map tables: audit_logs, experiments, experiment_assignments, experiment_results
None of these 4 tables appear in the Supabase snapshot SQL (no CREATE TABLE statement found)
These models reference each other: Experiment -> ExperimentAssignment -> ExperimentResult
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Run prisma migrate to create these 4 tables, or remove the models if they are not yet needed. The Experiment chain is self-contained and can be migrated atomically.

evidence: DB snapshot: deal_files has tenant_id column (NOT NULL, varchar)
DB snapshot: deal_products has tenant_id column (NOT NULL, varchar)
DB snapshot: agent_actions has tenant_id column (NOT NULL, varchar)
Prisma DealFile model has NO tenantId field
Prisma DealProduct model has NO tenantId field
Prisma AgentAction model has NO tenantId field
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add tenantId String to DealFile, DealProduct, and AgentAction Prisma models with @relation to Tenant. The DB already has these columns — Prisma is silently ignoring them, causing tenant isolation bypass in queries.

evidence: DB: activity_events.tenant_id character varying NOT NULL
Prisma: ActivityEvent.tenantId String? (nullable)
Nullability mismatch — Prisma allows null but DB rejects it
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change Prisma ActivityEvent.tenantId from String? to String to match DB NOT NULL constraint. Currently Prisma allows null values that will fail at DB level.

evidence: DB: contact_activities.tenant_id character varying NOT NULL
Prisma: ContactActivity.tenantId String? (nullable)
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change ContactActivity.tenantId from String? to String.

evidence: DB: security_events.tenant_id character varying NOT NULL
Prisma: SecurityEvent.tenantId String? (nullable)
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change SecurityEvent.tenantId from String? to String.

evidence: DB: message_records.tenant_id character varying NOT NULL
Prisma: MessageRecord.tenantId String? (nullable)
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change MessageRecord.tenantId from String? to String.

evidence: DB: tool_call_records.tenant_id character varying NOT NULL
Prisma: ToolCallRecord.tenantId String? (nullable)
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change ToolCallRecord.tenantId from String? to String.

evidence: DB: teams table has tenant_id column; team_members table has tenant_id column
Prisma: Team model has NO tenantId field; TeamMember model has NO tenantId field
These are core organizational models that must be tenant-scoped
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add tenantId String to Team and TeamMember Prisma models with @relation to Tenant.

evidence: DB: workspaces.plan column type is text
Prisma: Workspace.plan is String (varchar by default)
Minor type mismatch — text allows unlimited length vs varchar default
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add @db.Text annotation to Workspace.plan in Prisma to match the DB text type, or verify the implicit varchar mapping is acceptable.

evidence: 35 models have tenantId String field but no Tenant @relation declaration
Without @relation, Prisma cannot generate join queries and FK constraint is not enforced at ORM layer

Affected: LeadActivity, LeadNote, LeadFile, ActivityEvent, ContactActivity, MessageRecord, ToolCallRecord, Appointment, AppointmentAttendee, AppointmentCase, Case, CaseTask, SecurityEvent, TicketActivity, UserRoleAssignment, UserPermission, LeadAIInsight, ContactAIInsight, AutoResponseDraft, EmailRecord, ChatMessage, CallRecord, AgentAvailability, RoutingRule, RoutingAudit, CaseDocument, CaseDocumentACL, CaseDocumentAudit, Notification, NotificationPreference, NotificationTemplate, NotificationDLQ, LeadConversionAudit, ZepEpisodeUsage, ZepEpisodeAudit
affected_objects: 35 models with tenantId but no Tenant @relation
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add tenant Tenant @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) to each affected model and corresponding back-relations on the Tenant model. Prioritize Case, Appointment, Notification first.

evidence: Business-domain models missing tenantId entirely: Document, ChatConversation, Workspace, WorkspaceMember, WorkflowDefinition, BusinessRule, DashboardConfig, KPIDefinition, APIKey, WebhookEndpoint, RoutingRule
CLAUDE.md states: 'Almost all models need tenantId for multi-tenancy'
Without tenantId, repository queries could leak data across tenants
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add tenantId String to all business-domain models. Infrastructure/system models (HealthCheck, AlertIncident, APIVersion) may legitimately omit tenantId.

evidence: Opportunity.accountId references Account — both have tenantId but no cross-tenant FK guard
Task.contactId, Task.accountId reference Contact/Account across potential tenant boundaries
CalendarEvent.contactId references Contact — same risk
Without composite FK (tenantId + entityId), a bug could link records from different tenants
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add composite FK constraints that include tenantId to prevent cross-tenant references. E.g., Opportunity should reference Account by (tenantId, accountId) not just accountId.

evidence: TicketCategory has @@unique([name]) — global uniqueness means Tenant A and Tenant B cannot both create a category named 'Bug'
Should be @@unique([tenantId, name]) for multi-tenant isolation
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change @@unique([name]) to @@unique([tenantId, name]).

evidence: Most tenantId-scoped entities lack tenant-scoped uniqueness constraints
E.g., Account.email could collide across tenants without @@unique([tenantId, email])
affected_objects: Multiple models with tenantId but no composite unique constraints
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Audit all unique constraints to ensure they include tenantId where multi-tenant isolation is required.

evidence: WebhookEndpoint.secret: String — stored as plaintext varchar in DB
Webhook secrets are used for HMAC signature verification and should be encrypted at rest
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Encrypt webhook secrets at the application layer before storing, or use a vault/KMS integration. At minimum add a comment marking this field as requiring encryption.

evidence: CallRecord stores phoneNumber, recordingUrl, transcription — PII that may require encryption
No encryption markers or @db.Encrypted annotation present
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Evaluate whether CallRecord PII fields need application-layer encryption for GDPR/CCPA compliance.

evidence: IP addresses stored as plaintext String in: SecurityEvent.ipAddress, AuditLogEntry.ipAddress, CaseDocumentAudit.ip_address, NotificationDLQ (metadata may contain IPs), plus 3+ other models
IP addresses are personal data under GDPR
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Hash or encrypt IP addresses at rest. For analytics, store hashed IPs; for security forensics, use reversible encryption with key rotation.

evidence: ChainVersion.prompt and ChainVersion.systemPrompt stored as plaintext String
AI prompts may contain sensitive business logic, customer data templates, or PII extraction patterns
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Consider encrypting prompts at rest or implementing access controls on ChainVersion reads.

evidence: A consent/permission tracking model has permissionGranted Boolean @default(true)
Default-true consent violates privacy-by-default principles (GDPR Article 25)
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change default to false. Consent should be opt-in, not opt-out.

evidence: Contact.email has @@unique([email]) — global uniqueness across all tenants
An attacker in Tenant A can probe email existence by attempting to create contacts with known emails — failure reveals the email exists in another tenant
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change to @@unique([tenantId, email]) to prevent cross-tenant information leakage via uniqueness probing.

evidence: CaseDocument (line 3300) uses snake_case columns: tenant_id, version_major, created_at, etc. (24 fields)
CaseDocumentACL (line 3366) uses snake_case: document_id, tenant_id, principal_id, etc. (8 fields)
CaseDocumentAudit (line 3394) uses snake_case: document_id, tenant_id, event_type, etc. (7 fields)
All other 110+ models use camelCase Prisma convention
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Rename all snake_case columns to camelCase. Use @map() on each field if DB column names must stay snake_case. This is the most visually jarring inconsistency in the schema.

evidence: 5 Legal domain models use @default(uuid()): Case, CaseTask, CaseDocument, CaseDocumentACL, CaseDocumentAudit
108 other models use @default(cuid())
Mixed ID strategies cause developer confusion and incompatible ID formats in cross-domain joins
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Standardize on one ID strategy. cuid() is the established standard (108 models). Migrate the 5 uuid() models, or document the Legal domain exception clearly.

evidence: Decimal @db.Decimal(15,2): Account.revenue, Opportunity.value, DealProduct.unitPrice, DealProduct.totalPrice
Int (cents): PipelineSnapshot.value, SalesPerformance.revenue, DealRenewal.value, LeadAIInsight.estimatedValue, ContactAIInsight.lifetimeValue
Comparing Opportunity.value (Decimal $50,000) with DealRenewal.value (Int 5000000 cents) produces silent unit mismatch
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Pick one canonical money representation. Decimal @db.Decimal(15,2) for all monetary amounts (preferred for CRM) OR Int-in-cents for all. Document the convention clearly.

evidence: ConversationRecord.status: String @default('ACTIVE') — should be enum
ToolCallRecord.status: String @default('PENDING') — should be enum
ToolCallRecord.approvalStatus: String? — should be enum
ReportExecution.status: String — should be enum
All other status fields use typed enums
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Create proper Prisma enums for each: ConversationStatus, ToolCallStatus, ToolCallApprovalStatus, ReportExecutionStatus. String status fields lose compile-time safety.

evidence: 5 models use sentiment as String: LeadAIInsight, ContactAIInsight, TicketAIInsight, CallRecord, FeedbackSurvey
Sentiment enum already exists and is used correctly in LeadActivity and ContactActivity
String fields have inconsistent capitalization: 'Positive' vs 'positive'
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Replace all 5 sentiment String fields with the existing Sentiment enum. Drop-in replacement, no new enum needed.

evidence: ToolCallRecord has toolInput Json? AND inputParameters Json? (same purpose)
ToolCallRecord has toolOutput Json? AND outputResult Json? (same purpose)
ToolCallRecord has duration Int? AND durationMs Int? (both in ms)
Comments say 'Alternative field name' — incomplete migration
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Consolidate each pair: keep toolInput (drop inputParameters), keep toolOutput (drop outputResult), keep durationMs (drop duration). Migrate data first, then remove deprecated columns.

evidence: ZepEpisodeUsage uses lastUpdated @updatedAt — the only model deviating from updatedAt convention
All other 113 models with @updatedAt use the field name updatedAt
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Rename to updatedAt for consistency.

evidence: DomainEvent uses 'tenants' (plural) for Tenant @relation field
All other 100+ models use 'tenant' (singular)
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Rename to 'tenant' (singular). Schema-only change, no DB migration needed.

evidence: SecurityEventType enum (16 values) declared but never referenced by any model field
EventOutcome enum (4 values) declared but never referenced by any model field
SecurityEvent.eventType uses String instead of SecurityEventType enum
Prisma 5.22.0 may skip generating TS types for unreferenced enums
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Wire SecurityEventType to SecurityEvent.eventType (replace String with enum) and find a home for EventOutcome, or delete both unused enums.

evidence: Permission model has no @@map — defaults to PascalCase table 'Permission'
NotificationPreference model has no @@map — defaults to 'NotificationPreference'
ExperimentResult model has no @@map — defaults to 'ExperimentResult'
All other 110 models have explicit @@map with snake_case table names
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add @@map('permissions'), @@map('notification_preferences'), @@map('experiment_results').

evidence: ChainVersion has activatedAt/activatedBy pair and deprecatedAt/deprecatedBy pair
archivedAt DateTime? exists but has no corresponding archivedBy String?
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add archivedBy String? to complete the At/By symmetry pattern.

evidence: TicketNextStep.dueDate: String — stores display strings like 'Due Today'
All other dueDate fields (Task.dueDate, CaseTask.dueDate) are DateTime?
String dueDate cannot be sorted, compared, or used in SLA calculations
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Change to DateTime? and compute display labels at the application layer.

evidence: Ticket, SLABreach, TicketCategory all reference slaPolicyId with no @@index
SLA-related queries (breach lookups, policy filtering) will full-scan these tables
Ticket table is high-volume in CRM systems
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add @@index([slaPolicyId]) to Ticket, SLABreach, and TicketCategory models.

evidence: AgentAction model lacks tenantId in Prisma (DBA-002) and has no composite indexes
Without tenantId index, all agent-action queries must scan the full table
Agent actions are high-frequency in AI-powered CRM
confirmed_findings.recommendation: After adding tenantId (DBA-002), add @@index([tenantId, createdAt]) and @@index([tenantId, status]).

evidence: ChainVersion parent version traversal uses parentVersionId FK with no dedicated index
CaseDocument uses parent_version_id similarly without index
Version history lookups require traversing parent chains
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add @@index([parentVersionId]) to ChainVersion and @@index on parent_version_id for CaseDocument.

evidence: PerformanceMetric and APIUsageRecord are append-only high-volume tables
PerformanceMetric has @@index([tenantId]) and @@index([metricType]) but no composite time-range index
APIUsageRecord has @@index([tenantId]) and @@index([apiKeyId]) but no time-range index
Time-range queries (last 24h, last 7d) will be slow without @@index([tenantId, createdAt])
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Add composite indexes: @@index([tenantId, createdAt]) for time-range queries and consider partitioning or retention policies.

evidence: 5 models have tenantId String? (nullable) with @@index([tenantId])
Nullable columns in B-tree indexes cause NULL values to cluster, reducing index selectivity
Affected: ActivityEvent, ContactActivity, SecurityEvent, MessageRecord, ToolCallRecord
confirmed_findings.recommendation: Fix the nullability first (DBA-003 through DBA-007), then the indexes will be more efficient automatically.

evidence: ChatConversation.priority: String @default('normal') — 'normal' does not map to any existing priority enum
AIInsight.priority: String @default('medium') — could use TaskPriority enum
4 different priority enums exist: TaskPriority, TicketPriority, CasePriority, NotificationPriority
needs_review_findings.recommendation: Consider creating a shared Priority enum or mapping these to existing ones. May be intentionally different per domain.

evidence: AuditLogEntry uses 'timestamp', AIOutputReviewAudit uses 'timestamp', ChainVersionAudit uses 'performedAt'
Activity models use 'timestamp' conflating event time with record creation time
Convention is 'createdAt' for record creation
needs_review_findings.recommendation: Separate createdAt (record insertion) from occurredAt (business event time). Requires business context to determine if the distinction matters for these models.

evidence: CaseDocument uses deleted_at DateTime? — timestamp-based soft delete
DocumentStatus enum has DELETED value — status-based soft delete
TaskStatus and TicketStatus have ARCHIVED — status-based archival
No consistent pattern across the schema
needs_review_findings.recommendation: Standardize on one soft-delete approach. Needs architectural decision (ADR) to pick timestamp vs status enum pattern.

evidence: UserRoleAssignment has userId String with no User @relation
UserPermission has userId String with no User @relation
User model has no roleAssignments[] or permissionOverrides[] back-relations
needs_review_findings.recommendation: Add User @relation and back-relations for efficient RBAC lookups. Depends on whether RBAC queries go through User model or directly through assignment tables.

evidence: Multiple models have Unsupported('vector(1536)') and Unsupported('tsvector') columns
No HNSW or GIN indexes visible in Prisma schema for these columns
Vector similarity search and full-text search require specialized indexes
needs_review_findings.recommendation: Add HNSW indexes for vector columns and GIN indexes for tsvector columns via raw SQL migration. Verify these indexes exist at the DB level even if not in Prisma schema.

evidence: Audit tables (AuditLogEntry, SecurityEvent, DomainEvent, etc.) are append-only with no retention index
Without retention management, these tables will grow unbounded
needs_review_findings.recommendation: Add @@index([createdAt]) for efficient retention cleanup queries. Consider table partitioning for high-volume audit tables.

evidence: Notification model has 7 indexes: tenantId, userId, channel, status, priority, category, createdAt
Write amplification: every INSERT/UPDATE touches 7 B-tree indexes
Notifications are high-frequency write operations
needs_review_findings.recommendation: Evaluate which indexes are actually used by queries. Consider composite indexes to reduce total index count. May be acceptable if read patterns justify all 7.

evidence: Task model has leadId and opportunityId FK fields but no dedicated indexes
Task lookups by lead or opportunity may require full table scans
needs_review_findings.recommendation: Add @@index([leadId]) and @@index([opportunityId]) if these are common query patterns.

migration_backlog.description: Fix Prisma-DB tenantId drift: add missing tenantId to 3 models, fix 5 nullable mismatches, add tenantId to Team/TeamMember
Add missing performance indexes: slaPolicyId, AgentAction composites, parent version FKs, time-range composites
Create 4 missing DB tables (audit_logs, experiments, experiment_assignments, experiment_results) or remove Prisma models
Add Tenant @relation to 35 models missing it (prioritize Case, Appointment, Notification)
Replace String status/sentiment fields with proper enums, wire unused enums
Fix naming/convention issues: TicketCategory unique, Contact.email unique, ZepEpisodeUsage naming, DomainEvent.tenants, missing @@map, ChainVersion archivedBy, TicketNextStep.dueDate type
Architectural decisions needed: add tenantId to 11 models, CaseDocument snake_case migration, uuid/cuid standardization, money type standardization
Security hardening: encrypt webhook secrets, evaluate PII encryption, hash IPs, encrypt prompts, fix consent default
